title: Space Station Rescue
description: The space station's computer crashed! Solve engineering puzzles to restart each module before the oxygen runs out.

questions.text: 🚀 MODULE 1 — LIFE SUPPORT: The oxygen generator produces 2.5 liters per minute. How many liters will it produce in 4 minutes? Hurry — the crew is counting on you!
hints: Think of 2.5 as 2 + 0.5, then multiply each part by 4
questions.explanation: 2.5 × 4 = 10 liters. Oxygen flow restored! The crew can breathe easy — for now. Moving to Module 2...

questions.text: 🔧 MODULE 2 — POWER GRID: The station has 3 solar panels. Each panel generates 1/6 of the total power needed. What fraction of total power do all 3 panels provide together?
questions.explanation: 1/6 + 1/6 + 1/6 = 3/6 = 1/2. That's half the power restored! But we need more. Advancing to Module 3...

questions.text: ⚡ MODULE 3 — BACKUP BATTERY: The backup battery is at 45%. It drains 5% every hour. How many hours until it reaches 0%?
hints: How many times does 5 go into 45?
questions.explanation: 45 ÷ 5 = 9 hours remaining. That gives us time, but we need to work fast! Module 4 is next...

questions.text: 🛰️ MODULE 4 — COMMUNICATIONS: The radio signal bounces between 3 satellites. The total distance is 12,600 km split equally. How far apart is each satellite?
questions.explanation: 12,600 ÷ 3 = 4,200 km between each satellite. Signal locked! We can call for help now. On to Module 5...

questions.text: 🔩 MODULE 5 — HULL REPAIR: A hull breach is 3/8 of a meter wide. The repair patch covers 5/8 of a meter. How much extra patch extends beyond the breach?
questions.explanation: 5/8 - 3/8 = 2/8 = 1/4 meter. The patch fits with room to spare! Hull sealed. Module 6 awaits...

questions.text: 🌡️ MODULE 6 — TEMPERATURE CONTROL: The station is at -12°C. The heater raises the temperature by 3.5°C per cycle. After 4 cycles, what is the temperature?
questions.explanation: 3.5 × 4 = 14. Then -12 + 14 = 2°C. Temperature stabilized! The crew is warming up. Module 7 is critical...

questions.text: 🔑 MODULE 7 — SECURITY DOOR: The access code requires the first prime number greater than 20. Which number opens the door?
hints: A prime number is only divisible by 1 and itself
Check each number — is 21 divisible by 3?
questions.explanation: 23 is prime (only divisible by 1 and 23). 21 = 3×7, 22 = 2×11, 25 = 5×5. Door unlocked! Entering Module 8...

questions.text: 📡 MODULE 8 — NAVIGATION: The station orbits Earth every 90 minutes. How many complete orbits does it make in 24 hours?
questions.explanation: 24 hours = 1,440 minutes. 1,440 ÷ 90 = 16 orbits. Navigation systems tracking! Now for the tougher repairs...

questions.text: 🚨 SYSTEM 9 — ALERT! FUEL SYSTEM: The fuel tank is 3/4 full. After using 1/3 of what's left, what fraction of the tank still has fuel? Solve quickly — the engine needs to fire in 60 seconds!
questions.explanation: 1/3 of 3/4 = 1/4. So 3/4 - 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. Fuel system stabilized! Engine firing on schedule. System 10 needs attention...

questions.text: ⚠️ SYSTEM 10 — WATER RECYCLER: The recycler processes 0.75 liters per cycle. If the crew needs 18 liters per day, how many cycles must run?
questions.explanation: 18 ÷ 0.75 = 24 cycles. Water recycler programmed and running! The crew won't go thirsty. System 11 is failing...

questions.text: 🔬 SYSTEM 11 — LABORATORY: An experiment needs a ratio of 2:5 chemical mix. If you have 8 mL of chemical A, how much of chemical B do you need?
questions.explanation: 2:5 = 8:x → x = 8 × 5 ÷ 2 = 20 mL. Experiment saved from contamination! System 12 is overheating...

questions.text: 🌀 SYSTEM 12 — CENTRIFUGE: The centrifuge spins at 150 RPM. To separate the samples, it needs to reach 200% of its current speed. What speed is needed?
questions.explanation: 200% of 150 = 2 × 150 = 300 RPM. Centrifuge at full speed! Samples processing. System 13 has a breach...

questions.text: 🛡️ SYSTEM 13 — RADIATION SHIELD: The shield blocks 7/10 of radiation. A solar flare sends 500 units. How many units get through the shield?
hints: If 7/10 is blocked, then 3/10 gets through
questions.explanation: If 7/10 is blocked, 3/10 gets through. 3/10 × 500 = 150 units. Shield holding! Crew is safe. System 14 next...

questions.text: 🔋 SYSTEM 14 — SOLAR ARRAY: Panel A produces 12.6 kW, Panel B produces 8.75 kW, and Panel C is offline. What's the total power from A and B combined?
questions.explanation: 12.60 + 8.75 = 21.35 kW. Partial power restored! We still need Panel C, but this will keep us going. System 15 is critical...

questions.text: 🗺️ SYSTEM 15 — MAPPING: The station's rectangular observation window is 1.2 m long and 0.8 m wide. What is the area of the window?
hints: Area of a rectangle = length × width
questions.explanation: 1.2 × 0.8 = 0.96 m². Window dimensions logged for the replacement order. System 16 is sparking!

questions.text: 💡 SYSTEM 16 — LIGHTING GRID: 35% of the 120 lights on the station are broken. How many lights still work?
hints: If 35% are broken, then 65% still work
questions.explanation: 65% of 120 = 0.65 × 120 = 78 lights. Rerouting power to working lights! Almost done — only the hardest systems left...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 17 — CRITICAL! DOCKING PORT: A supply ship is approaching. Its cargo bay is a rectangular prism: 4 m long, 3 m wide, and 2.5 m tall. What volume of supplies can it hold?
questions.explanation: 4 × 3 × 2.5 = 30 m³. Docking port calibrated for the incoming supply ship! Alert 18 is flashing red...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 18 — CRITICAL! ESCAPE POD: The escape pod fits 2/3 of the crew. If 5/6 of those people need space suits, and there are 24 crew members total, how many suits are needed?
questions.explanation: 2/3 of 24 = 16 people fit in the pod. 5/6 of 16 — wait, let's recalculate: 5/6 × 16 = 80/6 ≈ 13.3, round up to 14 suits needed. Safety first — always round up! Alert 19 incoming...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 19 — CRITICAL! THRUSTER CONTROL: The station is drifting! Thruster A fires at a ratio of 3:4 with Thruster B. If Thruster B fires at 240 newtons, how much force does Thruster A provide?
questions.explanation: 3/4 = A/240 → A = 3 × 240 ÷ 4 = 180 newtons. Thrusters balanced! The station is stabilizing. One final alert...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 20 — FINAL SYSTEM! MAIN COMPUTER: The computer needs a prime factorization code. What is the prime factorization of 120? Enter the code to reboot the entire station!
hints: Start dividing 120 by the smallest prime: 2
questions.explanation: 120 = 2 × 60 = 2 × 2 × 30 = 2 × 2 × 2 × 15 = 2 × 2 × 2 × 3 × 5 = 2³ × 3 × 5. CODE ACCEPTED! All systems rebooted — the space station is fully operational! Mission complete, Commander! 🎉

title: Mission to Mars
description: You're navigating a spacecraft to Mars. Calculate trajectories, manage fuel, and solve problems to complete the mission!

questions.text: 🚀 MISSION LOG 1 — LAUNCH SEQUENCE: The rocket burns 3.75 tons of fuel per minute during launch. How much fuel is used in the first 8 minutes?
questions.explanation: 3.75 × 8 = 30 tons of fuel burned. Launch successful! You've cleared Earth's atmosphere. Mission Log 2 incoming...

questions.text: 📋 MISSION LOG 2 — CREW ASSIGNMENTS: The 18-person crew is split into teams with a ratio of 2:3:4 for navigation, engineering, and science. How many are on the science team?
hints: Science is 4 parts out of 9 total
questions.explanation: Total parts = 2 + 3 + 4 = 9. Each part = 18 ÷ 9 = 2 people. Science = 4 × 2 = 8 crew members. Teams assigned! Mission Log 3...

questions.text: 🛸 MISSION LOG 3 — TRAJECTORY CHECK: Your spacecraft has traveled 2/5 of the distance to Mars. If the total distance is 225 million km, how far have you gone?
hints: Divide 225 by 5 first, then multiply by 2
questions.explanation: 2/5 × 225 = 450/5 = 90 million km. You're making great progress! Mars is getting closer. Mission Log 4...

questions.text: 🌑 MISSION LOG 4 — ASTEROID DODGE: An asteroid is 15.8 km ahead. Your ship moves 2.3 km per second to the side. How far will you have moved laterally in 5 seconds?
questions.explanation: 2.3 × 5 = 11.5 km lateral movement. Asteroid dodged! That was close. Mission Log 5 coming in...

questions.text: 📊 MISSION LOG 5 — SUPPLY CHECK: You started with 480 food packs. After 30 days, 35% have been consumed. How many food packs remain?
questions.explanation: 65% of 480 = 0.65 × 480 = 312 packs remaining. Supplies are on track! Mission Log 6...

questions.text: 🔭 MISSION LOG 6 — TELESCOPE CALIBRATION: The telescope lens has a circular shape with a diameter of 14 cm. What is its radius?
questions.explanation: Radius = diameter ÷ 2 = 14 ÷ 2 = 7 cm. Telescope calibrated and focused on Mars! Mission Log 7 incoming...

questions.text: ⚡ MISSION LOG 7 — POWER CHECK: The ship uses 125 kWh per day. The solar panels generate 87.5 kWh. How much must the backup generator provide?
hints: Subtract what the panels provide from the total needed
questions.explanation: 125 - 87.5 = 37.5 kWh from the backup generator. Power systems balanced! Mission Log 8 ahead...

questions.text: 🗝️ MISSION LOG 8 — SECURE VAULT: The supply vault code is the least common multiple (LCM) of 6 and 8. What's the code?
hints: List multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24... Find the first match
questions.explanation: Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18, 24... Multiples of 8: 8, 16, 24... LCM = 24. Vault unlocked! Entering the medium-difficulty zone of the mission...

questions.text: 🚨 SYSTEM 9 — FUEL EFFICIENCY: Your ship travels 4.2 km per liter of fuel. You need to cover 378 km to reach a refueling station. How many liters do you need?
questions.explanation: 378 ÷ 4.2 = 90 liters. Fuel loaded and engines running efficiently! System 10 needs your attention...

questions.text: ⚠️ SYSTEM 10 — CARGO BAY: The cargo bay is a rectangular prism: 6 m × 4.5 m × 3 m. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: 6 × 4.5 = 27, then 27 × 3 = 81 m³. Cargo bay dimensions confirmed — everything fits! System 11 is overheating...

questions.text: 🔥 SYSTEM 11 — HEAT SHIELD: The heat shield temperature is rising at a rate of 15°C per minute. If it starts at 120°C and the maximum safe temperature is 345°C, how many minutes until it reaches the danger zone?
hints: Divide by the rate of increase
questions.explanation: 345 - 120 = 225°C difference. 225 ÷ 15 = 15 minutes. We have 15 minutes to cool the shield! System 12 is next...

questions.text: 🌀 SYSTEM 12 — GRAVITY SIMULATOR: The simulator needs to spin the crew module. If the module is a circle with a radius of 7 meters, what is the circumference? (Use π ≈ 22/7)
questions.explanation: C = 2 × 22/7 × 7 = 2 × 22 = 44 meters. Gravity simulator spinning at the correct circumference! System 13 alert...

questions.text: 📡 SYSTEM 13 — DEEP SPACE SIGNAL: A signal from Earth takes 12.5 minutes to reach you. If the signal travels at 300,000 km/s, how far are you from Earth in millions of km?
questions.explanation: 12.5 min = 750 seconds. 750 × 300,000 = 225,000,000 km = 225 million km. Signal distance calculated! System 14 is critical...

questions.text: 🔧 SYSTEM 14 — ENGINE REPAIR: A replacement part costs $2,400 on Earth. In space, parts cost 250% more. What is the space price?
hints: 250% more means the space price is 100% + 250% = 350% of original
questions.explanation: 350% of $2,400 = 3.5 × $2,400 = $8,400. Good thing we brought spares! Engine part installed. System 15...

questions.text: 🛰️ SYSTEM 15 — ORBITAL ENTRY: To enter Mars orbit, you need to reduce speed by 3/8. If your current speed is 24,000 km/h, what should your new speed be?
questions.explanation: 3/8 × 24,000 = 9,000. New speed = 24,000 - 9,000 = 15,000 km/h. Orbital entry speed achieved! System 16...

questions.text: 🧪 SYSTEM 16 — ATMOSPHERE ANALYSIS: Mars atmosphere is 95.3% CO₂, 2.7% nitrogen, and the rest is other gases. What percentage is other gases?
hints: All percentages must add up to 100%
questions.explanation: 100% - 95.3% - 2.7% = 2% other gases. Atmospheric data logged for the science team! Final challenge phase ahead...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 17 — CRITICAL! LANDING CALCULATIONS: The lander drops from 1,200 m altitude, descending 0.8 m every 0.25 seconds. How many seconds until landing?
hints: First find the rate per second: 0.8 ÷ 0.25
Then divide altitude by that rate
questions.explanation: Rate = 0.8 ÷ 0.25 = 3.2 m/s. Time = 1,200 ÷ 3.2 = 375 seconds. Landing trajectory locked! Alert 18 incoming...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 18 — CRITICAL! HABITAT CONSTRUCTION: The Mars habitat base is a square with a perimeter of 64 meters. Solar panels will cover 3/4 of the base area. How many square meters of solar panels are needed?
hints: Find the side length from the perimeter: 64 ÷ 4
Then find the area, then take 3/4 of it
questions.explanation: Side = 64 ÷ 4 = 16 m. Area = 16 × 16 = 256 m². Solar panels = 3/4 × 256 = 192 m². Habitat blueprints approved! Alert 19...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 19 — CRITICAL! WATER EXTRACTION: A Mars ice drill extracts 2.4 kg of ice per hour. Only 62.5% of the ice can be converted to drinkable water. How many kg of water do you get in 10 hours?
hints: First find total ice in 10 hours
questions.explanation: Total ice = 2.4 × 10 = 24 kg. Drinkable water = 62.5% × 24 = 0.625 × 24 = 15 kg. Water supply secured for the crew! Final alert...

questions.text: 🚨 ALERT 20 — FINAL MISSION! RETURN TRAJECTORY: The return trip is 3/5 longer than the trip to Mars, which took 210 days. The ship uses fuel at a rate inversely proportional — it will need 7/6 of the original 540 tons. How much fuel is needed for the return?
questions.explanation: Fuel needed = 7/6 × 540 = 3,780 ÷ 6 = 630 tons. Return trajectory plotted and fuel loaded! MISSION COMPLETE — You've successfully landed on Mars, built a habitat, and planned the return journey. Welcome to Mars, Commander! 🎉
